---
title: "shiny"
author: "Kimberly Olney, PhD"
date: "April 2026"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: cerulean
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = ".")
```

# Libraris, paths, colors
```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
source("bulk_RNAseq/scripts/file_paths_and_colours.R")
projectID <- "pigs_cellbender_after_recluster_harm_int_noise_removed_after_annotation_FINAL"
color.panel <- dittoColors()
```

# Libraries & paths
```{r libraries_variables}
library(ggdendro)
library(reticulate)
library(ShinyCell)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(plyr)
```

# Read RDS 
```{r read_pbj}
dataObject.annotated <- readRDS(paste0("snRNAseq/rObjects/",projectID,"_annotated.rds"))
```
```{r}
# =========================================================
# 1. Swap the incorrect Sample_ID values in the Seurat object
# =========================================================
sample_renaming_map <- c(
  "E1" = "S1",
  "E4" = "S2",
  "E6" = "S3",
  "E8" = "S6",
  "S1" = "E1",
  "S2" = "E4",
  "S3" = "E6",
  "S6" = "E8"
)

dataObject.annotated$Sample_ID <- plyr::mapvalues(
  x = as.character(dataObject.annotated$Sample_ID),
  from = names(sample_renaming_map),
  to = unname(sample_renaming_map),
  warn_missing = FALSE
)

# Keep 'sample' synchronized with Sample_ID
dataObject.annotated$sample <- dataObject.annotated$Sample_ID

# =========================================================
# 2. Prepare metadata table
# =========================================================
metadata2 <- metadata %>%
  mutate(sample = as.character(sample))

# Confirm one row per sample
stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(metadata2$sample))

# =========================================================
# 3. Define which sample-level columns to import
# =========================================================
sample_level_cols_to_add <- c(
  "sample",
  "sample_ID",
  "group",
  "condition",
  "pig_id",
  "dose_ml_per_hr",
  "weight_kg",
  "age_days",
  "duration_min",
  "start_heart_rate",
  "end_heart_rate",
  "start_temp",
  "end_temp",
  "sex",
  "start_SpO2",
  "end_SpO2",
  "start_MAP",
  "end_MAP"
)

sample_level_cols_to_add <- intersect(sample_level_cols_to_add, colnames(metadata2))

sample_lookup <- metadata2 %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(sample_level_cols_to_add))

# =========================================================
# 4. Remove outdated sample-level columns from Seurat metadata
# =========================================================
meta <- dataObject.annotated@meta.data %>%
  rownames_to_column("cell_barcode")

cols_to_drop <- intersect(sample_level_cols_to_add, colnames(meta))
meta <- meta %>% dplyr::select(-all_of(cols_to_drop))

# =========================================================
# 5. Join corrected sample-level metadata back by Sample_ID
# =========================================================
meta <- meta %>%
  left_join(sample_lookup, by = c("Sample_ID" = "sample"))

# Recreate sample so it exactly matches Sample_ID in the object
meta$sample <- meta$Sample_ID

# =========================================================
# 6. Sanity checks
# =========================================================
if (any(is.na(meta$group))) {
  unmatched <- unique(meta$Sample_ID[is.na(meta$group)])
  stop(
    paste0(
      "These Sample_ID values in the Seurat object did not match metadata$sample: ",
      paste(unmatched, collapse = ", ")
    )
  )
}

# Optional consistency checks
cat("\nSample_ID by group after update:\n")
print(table(meta$Sample_ID, meta$group))

# =========================================================
# 7. Put corrected metadata back into Seurat object
# =========================================================
meta <- meta %>% column_to_rownames("cell_barcode")
dataObject.annotated@meta.data <- meta

# =========================================================
# 8. Verify final result
# =========================================================
cat("\nUpdated Sample_ID counts:\n")
print(table(dataObject.annotated$Sample_ID))

cat("\nUpdated sample counts:\n")
print(table(dataObject.annotated$sample))

cat("\nUpdated group counts:\n")
print(table(dataObject.annotated$group))

cat("\nUpdated Sample_ID by group:\n")
print(table(dataObject.annotated$Sample_ID, dataObject.annotated$group))
```

```{r}
DefaultAssay(dataObject.annotated) <- "RNA"
dataObject.annotated <- NormalizeData(dataObject.annotated)
saveRDS(dataObject.annotated, "snRNAseq/rObjects/Ecoli_pigs_snRNA_object.rds")
```


```{r reorder_metadata}
metadata_sn <- colnames(dataObject.annotated@meta.data)
metadata_sn <- metadata_sn[c(1,36,4,2,3,11,50:65)]
sc.config <- createConfig(obj = dataObject.annotated, meta.to.include = metadata_sn)
```

# Output directory
```{r}
makeShinyApp(obj = dataObject.annotated,
             scConf = sc.config, 
             gene.mapping = TRUE,
             shiny.dir = paste0("snRNAseq/shiny_apps/Ecoli_snRNAseq_Olney_et_al_2026"),
             shiny.title = paste0("Ecoli & saline pigs"))
```
